Now, this Agreement is applicable for those erstwhile USO Towers for which the IP decides to offer 
to the Telecom Service Providers (erstwhile USPs) and the Telecom Service Providers opt to retain I 
use the same by way of executing Individual Site Agreement, after the expiry of the ·subsidy period' 
ofthe respective tower unless specified otherwise. 
The USO Towers, for which the subsidy period has expired before the execution of this Agreement, 
shall also be governed by the terms and conditions of this Agreem'-!nt from the date on which the 
subsidy period for the respective USO Tower expired subject to USP opting to retain I continue 
using those USO Towers beyond the subsidy period by signing of Individual Site Agreement 
The USO Towers for which the USPs decide not to opt /retain/use the same, by signing the 
Individual Site Agreement(ISA) after the expiry of the subsidy period shall also be governed by the 
tenns and conditions of this Agreement as under: 
i) 
In cases, where Exit notice had been given by the USP and the Sites had been 
switched off I not radiating, the USP shall clear the dues, as per this Agreement, up to 
the date of switch off or one month after issue of notice whichever is later. 
ii) 
In cases, where Exit notice has not been given, the USPs shall notify the IP of their 
intention to Exit from such Sites, in writing, after execution of this Agreement. 
iii) 
The IP shall within 15 days upon receipt of such written notification from the USP or 
signing of this Agreement, for cases where notice has already been given by the USP, 
shall raise final invoice/bills for such towers within 15 days and permit the USP to 
access the Site for the purpose of removal of its Telecom Equipment and vacation of 
such Site immediately after clearance of the bills etc by the USPs. The lock-in period 
shall not be applicable for such towers, if the Site is vacated within six (6) months 
after signing ofthe Master Sharing Agreement. The tower Sites which continue to be 
used beyond the period of six ( 6) months, as above, wi II be deemed as covered under 
this Agreement including the lock-in period clause and Telecom Service Provider shall 
sign the Individual Site Agreement for those towers Sites. The six (6) month period 
can be extended for a further period of maximum six months if the USP elects to 
extend and with mutual agreement and IP also agrees for the same. 
Hereinafter sometimes INFRA PROVIDER (IP) as IP and BSNL as Telecom Service Provider [TSP] 
may collectively be referred to as "Parties" or individually as "Party". 
NOW THEREFORE, IN CONSIDERATION of the premises and the mutual promises in this 
Agreement, the parties agree as follows: 
1. 
DEFINITIONS, AGREEMENT PARTS, PRIORITY 
1.1 
DEFINITIONS 
In this Agreement, including the Recitals, the following terms have the following 
meanings: 
1.1.1 
ADMINISTRATOR means the Administrator, Universal Service Obligation 
fund

1.1.2 AFFILIATE in relation to a Party means any person which, directly or 
indirectly, controls such Party or is controlled by such Party or is under 
common control with such Party. For the purposes of this Agreement, "Control" 
means the power and ability to direct the management and policies of the 
controlled company through ownership of or control of more than 50 (fifty) 
percent of the voting shares of the controlled company, either by contract or 
otherwise; 
1.1.3 
ANNEXURE/SCHEDULE means the Annexure/Schedule attached to this 
Agreement at the time of its execution and/or as may be attached thereafter on 
mutual consent. 
I. I .4 BASE TRANSCEIVER STATION (BTS) means a base transceiver station 
(BTS) which includes/ incorporates the equipment in a network that facilitates 
wireless communication between user equipment (UE) like mobile phones, 
computers etc. and a network. 
1.1.5 
CAPTIONS I HEADINGS: The Captions I Headings of this Agreement I 
Annexure I Schedules are inserted for convenience only and are not to be 
construed as part of this Agreement or the applicable Service Order or in any 
way limiting the scope or intent of its provision. The headings or the captions of 
the paragraph or chapters should not be used as a guide to interpret the 
paragraph or chapter. They shall be for reference only. 
1.1.6 COMMENCEMENT DATE shall have the same meaning as set forth in 
Clause 3. 
1.1. 7 COMPUTATION OF TIME: Where there is a reference to a period of time 
consisting of a number of days or calendar months after a specified day, the 
number of days and months, respectively, shall be counted from, but should not 
include, the specified day of the month from which the count started. If the end 
day of the period of time so computed falls on a non-Business Day, then the end 
day of the period shall be the next Business Day. 
1. 1.8 DIFFICULT CLUSTER: As per details given in Schedule 6 as identified by 
USOF Administrator; 
1.1.9 
EXIT means physical removal of Telecom Service Provider's (TSPs) all 
Telecom Equipment (TE) from telecom Site; 
1.1.1 0 EXIT AMOUNT means the amount payable in the event of termination of an 
Individual Site Agreement before the prescribed lock-in period. 
1.1.11 TELECOM SERVICE PROVIDER ITSPI means any telecom operator other 
than INFRA PROVIDER (IP) and including erstwhile Universal Service 
Providers, sharing the Passive Infrastructure at the Site. 
I. I .12 GOVERNING PRINCIPLES: The Services offered by INFRA PROVIDER 
(IP) shall be governed by Scope of Services and Service Level Agreement, as 
per Schedule I & Schedule 2 respectively, which shall be executed by the 
,Parties simultaneously with the execution of this Agreement.

1.1.24 OTHER TERMS: Other terms used but not defined in this Agreement shall 
have the meaning generally understood in the telecommunications industry. 
1.1.25 QUALITY OF SERVICE: "Quality of Service": is evaluated on the basis of 
uptime availability of Passive Infrastructure which is 98.5% per Site in "Normal 
Cluster" and 96.5% per Site in "Difficult Cluster", detennined on a monthly 
basis. 
1.1.26 RECITALS: The recitals to and in this Agreement form an integral part of the 
Agreement and are intended to assist in the interpretation of this Agreement. 
1.1.27 SERVICES: means provisioning of Site along with Passive Infrastructure 
facilities built thereon and the related operations and maintenance services as 
more fully described in the Schedules I & 2. 
1.1.28 TELECOM EQUIPMENT (TE): means a Radio Frequency (RF) antennae 
system, microwave or transmission antennae systems, BTS, feeder cables and 
any other active equipment used for wireless communication services that may 
be installed by the Telecom Service Provider in accordance with this Agreement. 
1. 1.29 TERRITORY: Means the telecom circle(s) of India in which the Infrastructure 
Provider and Telecom Service Providers provide services. 
I. I .30 TOWER SLOT: Means a specific location on a tower where antennas that 
transmit I receive signals related to a particular BTS can be fitted. 
1.1.3 1 UNDERSTANDING: The Parties acknowledge that the draftsmanship of this 
Agreement was a cooperative effort by both the Parties who were represented 
by counsel and that this Agreement shall not be construed for or against either 
Party, but this Agreement shall be interpreted in accordance with general tenor 
of the language in an effort to reach an equitable result. 
1.1.32 UNIVERSAL SERVICE PROVIDER (USP): means such entities like BSO I 
CMTS I UASL Licensees, which had entered into an Agreement with the 
Administrator for provision of telecom services under Part-B of the USO 
scheme. USPs have also been referred as Telecom Service Providers in this 
agreement

2.1 
INFRA PROVIDER (IP) OBLIGATIONS 
2.1.1 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall provide existing Infrastructure Sites including the 
Passive Infrastructure and its operation and maintenance services on shared basis to the 
Telecom Service Provider as per Schedule I and Schedule 2 of this Agreement. 
2.1.2 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall be fully responsible for the watch and ward, maintenance 
and operations of the Infrastructure Site and its Passive Infrastructure as specified in the 
Service Level Agreement annexed hereto. 
2.1.3 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall be responsible for proper upkeep of the electric meters 
and the DG sets. 
2.1.4 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall undertake maintenance activities of Passive 
Infrastructure at mutually agreed planned outage periods. 
2.1.5 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall be responsible for the statutory compliances and 
liasioning with municipal I government bodies in connection with the Infrastructure Sites, 
Passive Infrastructure and Services covered under this Agreement. 
2.1.6 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall be responsible for the payment of statutory levies I 
municipal charges and monthly consumption charges of power and fuel. 
2.1.7 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall ensure that appropriate land purchase agreements/lease 
agreements/license agreements as required under Applicable Law are in place for the 
Infrastructure Sites during the provisioning of Services, as contemplated here under this 
Agreement. 
2.1 .8 Should an additional external telecom service provider be offered an existing Site 
where Telecom Service Provider is operating, and such external telecom service provider shall 
require alteration of the existing Site, such 
alteration shall be carried out by INFRA 
PROVIDER (IP) (including changing of position of the TE) 
provided that INFRA 
PROVIDER (IP) shall inform the Telecom Service Provider about the necessary alterations at 
least 15 days in advance and shall coordinate and cooperate with the Telecom Service 
Provider so that any such alterations shall not adversely affect the quality and performance of 
TE of the Service Provider and these alterations shall be performed with minimum 
disturbance of the activities of the Telecom Service Provider on the Site and at no cost to the 
Telecom Service Provider, either directly or indirectly. 
2.1.9 The detailed list of the erstwhile USO Towers infrastructure Sites covered under this 
agreement along with LAT I LONG details are as given in Schedule 4. 
2.1.1 0 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall provide the required EMF signages as per the 
specifications of the DOT I TEC and maintain the same on regular basis at mutually agreed 
rates. Any penalty imposed by DoT/TERM, TRAI on USP on account of non-availability of 
the signages after its fixing and during the period for which the advance payment for 
maintenance has been made by the Service Provider, shall be payable by the Infra Provider 
and shall be adjusted against IP's payables. Any other requirement related to Passive 
Infrastructure imposed by the DOT/TEC etc shall be mutually discussed and action shall be 
taken as per r:putually agreed terms and conditions .

2.1.11 INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall provide uninterrupted accesses to all Sites to the TSP. 
A failure by IP to provide such access for more than seven (7) days except the cases which 
falls under the Force Majeure shall be considered a material breach of this Agreement 
entitling Telecom Service Provider to terminate the relevant Site Agreement without liability 
and without payment of any Exit Amounts. 
2.2 TELECOM SERVICE PROVIDER OBLIGATIONS 
2.2.1 The Telecom Service Provider agrees to utilize such Passive Infrastructure on shared 
basis with INFRA PROVIDER (IP) and also with other external telecom service providers of 
INFRA PROVIDER (IP). 
2.2.2 Subject to other clauses of this Agreement, the Telecom Service Provider shall use the 
Infrastructure Site only for the purpose of installation, operation and maintenance of its TE 
and use the said Site and its Passive Infrastructure for telecommunication activities. 
2.2.3 The Telecom Service Provider shall carry out the installation ofTE at the Sites and shall 
also maintain the TE in proper operating condition and maintain the same as to appearance 
and safety. The Telecom Service Provider shall ensure that operations of its TE does not 
interfere, obstruct or restrict use of the Site by INFRA PROVIDER (IP) or any other user of 
the Site and do not cause any harm /nuisance to public in which case both the Parties shall 
mutually work towards resolution of the same. The Telecom Service Provider shall allow 
access to its TE at any Sites only to its designated employees/ agents (''representatives") and 
shall communicate the list of such representatives in respect of each Site to INFRA 
PROVIDER (IP). The Telecom Service Provider shall be solely responsible for all acts and/or 
omissions of the representatives at the Sites. 
2.2.4 During the commissioning of TE and subsequent operations and maintenance of the 
TE, the Telecom Service Provider shall ensure that all the safety measures as mandated I 
required I desirable for the execution of the job, are properly taken care of by its employees 
and Telecom Service Provider shall at all times comply with its HSW Policy. (The copy of 
HSW policy is attached to this Agreement as Schedule 8.) 
2.2.5 The Telecom Service Provider shall at all times comply with Applicable Law relevant to 
the TE and, if requested by the IP, shall reasonably assist the IP with requests from any legal 
or statutory body. 
2.2.6 The Telecom Service Provider shall hold all necessary licenses, permits or other 
authorizations in relation to the sharing of Infrastructure Sites and provisioning of the 
telecom services. 
2.3 
COMMON OBLIGATIONS OF PARTIES 
2.3.1 Each Party shall ensure that during the operation and maintenance of its equipment 
installed on Infrastructure Site, it shall not obstruct/impede/hinder the operation of other 
Party's active equipment I TE installed at the same Site. In case of such obstruction or 
damage, the Party causing such obstruction or damage will be liable to compensate any 
expense to restQre/repair the same.

IX. In the event of any defect/failure of Passive Infrastructure at any of the Sites, the 
Infrastructure Provider's nominated representative shall attend the Site to rectify the 
fault. 
It is agreed between the parties that Infrastructure Provider shall attend all-
critical alarms, which can lead to outage, within a maximum time of 2.5 hrs plus 
traveling time of maximum 2 hrs for remote sites between 6 AM and I 0 P.M and 
within 3.5 hrs plus traveling time of maximum 2 hrs for remote Sites between I 0 P.M 
and 6 AM, from the time of monitoring of such defect by the Infrastructure Provider's 
nominated representative. In case, there is more than one alarm at any Site(s) falling 
under same technician, then the priorities to attend the same shal I be decided by the 
INFRA PROVIDER (IP), whose decision will be final and binding in this respect. 
x. For effective monitoring and to minimize disputes in this regard. the Telecom Service 
Provider shall allow INFRA PROVIDER (IP)'s representatives for continuous online 
monitoring of the alarms for AC/DC failures in their NOC at circJe headquarters. 
xi. Proper arrangement shall be made for fire detection and fire extinguisher system for the 
installed equipment as per fire safety norms applicable for that location. 
xii. As and when the IP decides to provide the DC power, the uptime determination 
methodology shall be reviewed through mutual agreement. 
2.0 
ADDITIONAL RESPONSIBILITIES OF INFRA PROVIDER (IP) 
I. 
INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall be responsible for watch and ward, maintenance, 
operation of the Passive Infrastructure assets at Sites 
2. 
INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall ensure that the Identified Assets such as DG, Electrical 
Wiring, and Commercial Power are maintained in good working condition. 
3. 
INFRA PROVIDER (IP) shall be the single point of contact with the Site landlord, and 
all other local authorities. However, the Telecom Service Provider shall be responsible 
for obtaining SACF A clearance for frequency use. 
3.0 
EFFECTIVE DATE OF COMMENCEMENT OF RENTALS 
The effective date for an Individual Site for the commencement of rentals shall be the 
"Commencement Date" as mentioned in the "commencement date" in Clause-3 of the main 
agreement. 
4.0 
PAYMENTS FOR INVOICES 
The Telecom Service Provider shall pay to INFRA PROVIDER (IP)/Infrastructure Provider 
amounts due on the Invoice (s) and Debit Note (s) raised as mentioned below: 
a) 
An invoice of the previous month is to be raised by the IP in the first week of the 
current month and thereafter I 00% payment of IP fe.e and, 80% of energy and fuel charges 
shall be made within 30 days. 
b) 
Remaining 20 % payment of energy and fuel charges shall be made within 30 days 
after the execution of"Outage Sign-Off'', jointly agreed by both the parties. 
c) If the timeline of the payment mentioned in a) and b) above is not adhered to by the 
Telecom Service Provider, in that case the Telecom Service Provider is liable to pay late fee @ 
18% per annum to J1~A PROVIDER (IP).

OBLIGATION OF TELECOM SERVICE PROVIDER 
The obligations of the Telecom Service Provider include BTS, MW/ OFC connectivity, RF

6.0 
OUTAGE PENALTY: 
Outage penalty for Downtime at the individual Site shall be calculated as follows: 
For every month of operation of a Site, power outage duration as applicable beyond agreed 
downtime as in Para I (iii) & (iv) above, the Telecom Service Provider shall be entitled to 
claim/recover outage penalty as agreed compensation at rates mentioned below: 
A) At rate of Rs. 200/- per Hour for the downtime up to 24 Hours beyond prescribed limit in 
a month as in Para 1.0 (iii) & (iv) above. 
B) For the downtime beyond 24 hours beyond prescribed limit in a month as in Para 1.0 (iii) 
& (iv) above -Proportionate IP Fee of that month will be deducted for the remaining period 
subject to Minimum Penalty as mentioned in A above and limited to the one month IP Fee. 
C) If any Site remains down for more than 240 hours in a month for consecutive 4 months, 
and Telecom Service Provider does not opt to exit that particular Site for not meeting uptime 
SLA as per Clause 4.4 of the Master Sharing Agreement, in that case Telecom Service 
Provider shall be entitled to recover as agreed liquidated damage [LD]. at the rate of I 0% of 
the outage penalty calculated in A & B above, additionally. 
Both the parties shall jointly assess the quantum of compensation as per sign off details and 
in the case of dispute, INFRA PROVIDER (IP)/Telecom Service Provider shall substantiate by 
producing the sufficient evidence by way of documentation to each other's satisfaction. 
Above penalty shall not be applicable in Force Majeure conditions.

SCHEDULE-S 
Safetv, Health and Environment Policy 
On behalf of [the Vendor's Official name], hereinafter known as the "Vendor'", this is to certify on 
this __ day of 
2014 that: 
1. 
Vendor shall not use any form of forced, bonded or involuntary labour, and workers shall not 
he obliged to pay any deposit as a condition of work. 
2. 
Workers at the Site maintained by the Vendor shall not be su~ject to physical or verbal abuse 
or threats or intimidation of any description. 
3. 
Workers employed by the Vendor at the sites shall not be required to work extreme hours or 
work without adequate rest periods. 
4. 
No child or under age labour shall be used on the cell sites by Vendor. All workers have 
reached the age of 15, or have reached the minimum legal working age under national law, 
whichever age is the higher. 
5. 
Workers under the age of 18 shall not work at night or under potentially hazardous conditions. 
Vendor shall have adequate worker management systems for young workers in place. 
6. 
The cell sites maintained by Vendor shall, at all times, be safe and hygienic with an adequate 
number of safe and accessible fire exits from all buildings and workers shall have access to 
drinking water. 
7. 
Workers· life or limb shall not be endangered due to the use of dangerous machinery, unsafe 
building structure or layout, or hazardous chemicals. Where serious or fatal accidents have 
occurred Vendor shall demonstrate, to the satisfaction of Idea Cellular Ltd, that all appropriate 
steps have been taken to prevent similar accidents occurring in the future. 
8. 
Vendor shall not engage in bribery, corruption or other similar unethical practices in order to 
gain competitive advantage. 
9. 
Vendor shall comply with all national health and safety laws with regard to employment of 
laborers on the cell sites. 
10. Workers shall not be employed by Vendor on cell sites in potentially hazardous conditions 
without regular adequate safety training or supervision. Training shall include emergency 
evacuation procedures and, for appropriate designated personnel, first aid. 
Authorized Signatory of INFRA PROVIDER (JP)

ANNEXURE-I 
(Relevant Extract of BSNL CO letter No.61-20/2011-Infra Sales/Pt.IWB Dt 09.01.2012) 
Subject: Payment of energy bills to IP on the basis of "Fixed Hourly Energy Charges" -
issuing procedure and guidelines and the authorization to the circles. 
I. The circles are authorized to devise a suitable methodology for payment of energy bills to IP on 
the basis of fixed energy charges by forming joint committee including concerned representatives 
from both IP and TSP sides as under: 
i. GM/CE(E) dealing with generation of IP bills 
ii. DGM/SE (Electrical) dealing with generation of IP bills (Convener) 
iii. DGM (Finance) 
iv. Members to be nominated by TSP (Max. 3 nos.) 
2. The above committee may arrive at a suitable methodology on the basis of following guidelines: 
1. 
For calculation of load attributable to TSP, weighted average of load should be worked 
out for Indoor and Outdoor type BTS. Battery charging load should not be added in total 
load attributable to TSP rather battery discharge hours should be added in EB availability 
hours and/or DG run hours. It may be assumed that charging of the batteries is carried out 
on EB supply, to the extent of availability of EB supply and should be accounted 
appropriately. 
11. 
For electricity consumption charges: On the basis of load attributable to TSP (in KW), 
consumption of electricity per hour (in KWH) should be worked out for payment of 
electricity charges as per prevailing electricity tariff in the circle. 
111. 
For Diesel consumption charges: The consumption per hour (CPH) may be arrived at 
taking into account the average load attributable to TSP, the total load at IP site etc. The 
optimum dimensioned capacity of DG set (at least 15 KVA DG set) as per the total load 
at site, may be taken for CPH calculation. 
iv. 
For arriving at electricity availability hour and DG running hours, hired IP site may be 
suitably categorized as per availability of electricity at different locations in the circle. 
The committee has also to consider EB availability changes during the year at the time of 
annual review. 
v. 
The circle should review the energy charges payment methodology every year in 
consultation with TSP. 
vi. 
The committee appointed at circles should also finalize the list of necessary minimum 
documents, if any to be submitted by IP with the energy bills as per the energy charges 
payment methodology finalized by the committee and adopted in the circles. 
VII. 
The circles may further fine tune their payment procedures! methodology in view of the 
above guidelines. 
viii. 
Recommendation given by the above committee should be approved by the circle head in 
consultation with IF A.
